summary: The actual screenshot shows a game mode selector with a single mode card and navigation buttons on a dark starfield background, but it deviates from the wireframe and requirements by having only one mode card instead of two, a narrow left-aligned panel instead of a wide framed deck, and differing text and header elements. The wireframe itself is partially suitable as it captures style but not structural details.

correctElements.evidence: Both actual and wireframe feature a dark starfield background.
Thin green panel borders are present in both.
'Select Game Mode' header is visible in both, matching the screen description.
Continue/Primary Action and Back buttons are present under the main content.

incorrectElements.expected: Two large mode cards in the main panel.
incorrectElements.actual: Only one mode card ('Multiplayer') is present.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: The screen description requires two mode cards, but the actual UI shows only one, missing options like single-player.

incorrectElements.expected: Wide framed command deck with fixed screen-relative geometry.
incorrectElements.actual: Narrow left-aligned panel occupying less than half the screen width.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: The panel is not wide as described, leading to poor use of space and deviation from the 'wide framed command deck' requirement.

incorrectElements.actual: CONTINUE and BACK in actual, but wireframe shows PRIMARY ACTION and BACK.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Labels differ from wireframe placeholders, potentially confusing validation of exact UI behavior.

incorrectElements.expected: Header at top as per description, without extra title.
incorrectElements.actual: Wireframe has 'RiskyStars Fleet command interface' at top, but actual does not.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Extra header in wireframe not present in actual, indicating inconsistency in layout validation.

incorrectElements.actual: Actual has specific mode description, wireframe has placeholder description text inside panel.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe uses descriptive text block instead of actual UI elements, limiting precise comparison.

incorrectElements.actual: Appears to match, but potential DPI/scaling differences not verifiable from image alone.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: As per instructions, include at least one residual risk; inability to confirm exact pixel dimensions and scaling in audit introduces validation limitation.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe captures the overall style (background, borders, terminal-style) and provides a baseline, but it uses placeholder text and does not visually depict the two mode cards or wide deck structure, making it insufficient for precise layout validation against the screen description.
recommendedWireframeChanges: Add visual representations of two large mode cards with placeholders for titles and descriptions.
Specify exact positions and dimensions for panels and controls to enforce fixed geometry.
Replace placeholder button labels with expected ones like 'Continue'.
